target: Orphan ISS verification batch 1: prose format fix + falsifiability status label + 5 OBE resolutions

patches.description: ISS-1622: Normalize mixed numeral/word form. 'five-to-twenty-five' mixes word form with the numeral 'zero' and is inconsistent with the TLDR which uses '5–25 nT'. Fix: '5–25 nT' (en-dash, matching HTML entity – already used in TLDR). Qualitative meaning unchanged.

ISS-1658: Change falsifiability condition 2 status from 'partial' to 'pending'. The eclipse is Aug 12, 2026 — it has not occurred, so awarding 'partial' credit before data arrives is premature. 'pending' is the correct status label for an untested live prediction.

patches.find_pattern: aria-label="Status: 1 partial; eclipse August 12, 2026 approaches the standard with methodological issues"
patches.replace_pattern: aria-label="Status: 1 pending; eclipse August 12, 2026 live test approaching"
patches.description: ISS-1658 (continued): Update aria-label to match new 'pending' status and remove premature 'approaches the standard' language.
ISS-1658 (continued): Update displayed text from '1 partial' to '1 pending'.

obe_resolutions.reason: ISS-1624 reported SEC-4.2.2 TLDR was ~85 words / 3 sentences (EXP-253 version). Current TLDR reads as 2 sentences / ~68 words ('The dome's eclipse argument depends on a specific claim about what standard physics predicts — and that claim is wrong. The dome's published falsification criterion treats globe predicts 0 nT as the test; standard ionospheric physics (Chapman 1933) actually predicts 5–25 nT, confirmed across dozens of eclipses over nearly a century, and one of the dome's own baseline inputs already sits outside its ±3 nT globe window.'). Already within acceptable range. OBE from subsequent edits.
ISS-1639 flagged awkward third clause in SEC-4.2.2 TLDR (curmudgeon c2 on post-EXP-253 state). Current TLDR has no third clause — it is 2 clauses and reads cleanly. OBE from subsequent TLDR revision.

ISS-1640 flagged awkward §4.2.7 forward-reference parenthetical in SEC-4.2.2 body (curmudgeon c2). Current text reads: '(A second, independent failure appears in §4.2.7: the dome's own published parameters produce a totality zone roughly 1.3 km wide — about 1/220 the observed path of totality — via an argument that does not depend on the misstatement established here.)'. This is clear, well-structured prose. OBE from subsequent revision.

ISS-1542 flagged nested-parenthetical readability in WIN-013 Van Camp citation (curmudgeon c4). The cited problematic form was '(Van Camp et al., 2001 — the dome cites Van Camp 1999 (the eclipse year); the paper appeared in 2001, two years after the eclipse, ...)'. Current WIN-013 text reads: 'the dome's printed citation (Van Camp et al. 2001, Phys. Earth Planet. Inter., DOI 10.1023/A:1013940928637) does not resolve.' — the nested triple-reference structure is gone. OBE from subsequent WIN-013 revision.

ISS-1667 flagged missing km units on second r= occurrence in WIN-044. Current text: 'BOU reference station that anchors the ratio moved from r = 5,715 km (V50.6) to r = 9,983 km (V51)'. Both occurrences have km units. OBE from subsequent WIN-044 edit.
